_doc: CANONICAL program state — the SINGLE source for the auto-generated status blocks. Edit THIS, then run `python3 tools/render_state.py --write` to refresh every <!-- BEGIN GENERATED:* --> block across the context docs (README, SESSION_BOOTSTRAP, SESSION_CHECKPOINT, EXPERIMENT_RUNBOOK §0.3, PROGRESS ⑤). Never hand-edit between the GENERATED markers. This is the 'generate-what's-mechanical' half of the anti-drift design (the other half = narrative fingerprints in tools/closeout_fingerprints.json). The pre-commit hook blocks commits where these blocks are stale vs this file.
north_star: F1 — prove/falsify the 'LLM-as-Database' spec is implementable BEFORE it is built; deliver a ready / not-ready-with-conditions determination. Falsification-first.
current_latest: D-B3N-1 (B3 in-weight-necessity DECISION — highest-stakes F1 architecture item, TAKEN, then SPEC-GROUNDED): in-weight is NOT contractually required (its 'native-knowing' value, spec line 90, is a paradigm PREFERENCE; a structured side-store meets every ENFORCED read invariant given reliable routing). VERDICT keyed to the SPEC's OWN write model (§8.3/§8.4/§8.7/§8.10) = a COMPACTION-BOUNDED HYBRID (batch genesis + bounded incremental per-commit overlays + periodic compaction to a clean anchor), NOT batch-only. In-weight is VIABLE for that hybrid under TWO conditions, both from our own work: (1) the §8.7 drift trigger must be CONCENTRATION-AWARE (our D-D1-2 k≤1 amendment), not the spec's count-only 1500/8000; (2) compaction must fire before the clean envelope is exceeded. Side-store is preferred ONLY for high-churn-online editing beyond the spec's bounded overlays. Genesis core clean (A1+B3+E1; 3B/N≤100); corruption is incremental-path-only — not counted against the batch core. Reasoned position (no pre-registered falsifier), NOT a PASS. Reviewed: advisor ×2 + gpt-5.5 FIX-FIRST + OPERATOR redirect-to-spec (the spec, primary source, settles the write profile). docs/B3_IN_WEIGHT_NECESSITY_DECISION.md. Next-arc: 7B numeric-transfer (OQ-W1) → CP2 → write F1 as the conditioned hybrid.
next_actions: 7B numeric-threshold transfer via the proven determinism path (OQ-W1 cross-model) — sets the concentration-aware §8.7 threshold value cross-model (verdict condition 1)
CP2 query-schema build-items (L1 triple-readback + 5 query families + violates-rejection — contract-readiness)

→ F1 reconciliation: write B3 as 'in-weight viable for the spec's compaction-bounded hybrid under 2 conditions' (concentration-aware §8.7 amendment + compaction cadence); author the §8.7 amendment as a concrete spec change; resolve §1.1 dims (auditability/governance/security/routing/cost)
Operator CALIBRATION (not a fork): realistic incremental write RATE → sets compaction cadence + whether the high-churn-online side-store row ever applies
f1_verdict: NOT delivered. Deployment data-path spine PROVEN-FOR-SCOPE (recipe→A1 batch-clean→B3 Q4_K_M→E1·A CPU-serve; 3B / N≤100). B3 architecture decision TAKEN + SPEC-GROUNDED (D-B3N-1): the spec's write model (§8.3/§8.7/§8.10) is a COMPACTION-BOUNDED HYBRID; in-weight is viable for it under 2 conditions (concentration-aware §8.7 = our k≤1 amendment; compaction cadence). Side-store only for high-churn-online beyond the spec. Remaining blocks: CP2 (contract) + 7B numeric cross-model transfer (OQ-W1, sets condition-1 threshold) + the §1.1 open dims (auditability/governance/security/routing/cost).
